message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracking state

Update .beads/issues.jsonl with latest issue state changes from ongoing
development work. This keeps the local bead database synchronized with
actual progress made across the project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"bd-3620","title":"Add time-based eviction and FD health monitor tests","description":"## Context
The EMFILE fix (GH#89, commits 61bb6d1 and 81dd55f) replaced the broken sys.getrefcount() eviction
heuristic with time-based cleanup and added a periodic FD health monitor. While test_repo_cache.py
was updated for the new eviction model, several aspects lack dedicated test coverage:

### Missing test coverage:

#### A. Time-based eviction edge cases (add to test_repo_cache.py)
1. **test_eviction_grace_period_boundary**: Repo evicted exactly at EVICTION_GRACE_SECONDS ago
   should be closed (boundary test, currently only tests "far past" and "just now")
2. **test_mixed_grace_period_evictions**: Mix of expired and non-expired repos in evicted list.
   Verify only expired ones are closed, non-expired retained.
3. **test_cleanup_idempotent_after_force**: After force=True clears everything, subsequent
   _cleanup_evicted() returns 0 and doesn't crash on empty list
4. **test_put_triggers_cleanup_of_expired**: When put() evicts a repo, _cleanup_evicted runs.
   If there are already-expired repos in the evicted list, they should be cleaned up too.

#### B. proactive_fd_cleanup tests (new test file or section)
1. **test_proactive_cleanup_sufficient_headroom**: When headroom >= threshold, returns 0
2. **test_proactive_cleanup_low_headroom_force_eviction**: When headroom < threshold,
   _cleanup_evicted(force=True) is called
3. **test_proactive_cleanup_critical_headroom_clears_cache**: When headroom < threshold//2
   after eviction, full cache clear happens
4. **test_proactive_cleanup_unsupported_platform**: When get_fd_headroom returns -1, returns 0

#### C. FD health monitor (tests/test_fd_health.py)
The _worker_fd_health() coroutine in http.py. Consider extracting core logic for testability.

1. **test_fd_health_critical_triggers_aggressive_cleanup**: Mock get_fd_usage to return
   headroom < 15%, verify proactive_fd_cleanup called with threshold=limit
2. **test_fd_health_low_triggers_proactive_cleanup**: Mock headroom < 20% > 15%,
   verify cleanup with threshold=int(limit*0.25)
3. **test_fd_health_warning_only_logs**: Mock headroom < 30% > 20%, verify warning
   logged but no cleanup triggered
4. **test_fd_health_healthy_no_action**: Mock headroom > 30%, verify no warnings or cleanup

## Implementation notes
- Use time.monotonic() mocking via unittest.mock.patch for precise grace period testing
- Use mock for get_fd_usage/get_fd_headroom for FD health tests
- The FD health monitor tests may require extracting _worker_fd_health logic into a
  standalone async function (minor refactor for testability)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 4 new time-based eviction edge case tests added to test_repo_cache.py
- [ ] 4 proactive_fd_cleanup tests in test_repo_cache.py or new file
- [ ] 4 FD health monitor tests in tests/test_fd_health.py
- [ ] All 12 new tests pass: pytest tests/test_repo_cache.py tests/test_fd_health.py -v
- [ ] Grace period boundary test uses time.monotonic mocking (not real delays)
- [ ] FD health tests mock get_fd_usage at appropriate level
- [ ] Existing 53+ tests still pass: pytest --tb=short","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:16.990923725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:26.591789479Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3620","depends_on_id":"bd-asnu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:15.446276533Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3sq","title":"Update all dependencies to latest stable versions","description":"## Python Dependency Update for mcp_agent_mail

### Steps
1. `cd /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail`
2. Review `pyproject.toml` for pinned versions
3. `uv lock --upgrade` -- update all deps to latest compatible
4. `uv sync` -- install updated deps
5. `uv run pytest` -- run test suite
6. `uv run python -m mcp_agent_mail.cli serve-http --help` -- verify CLI works
7. Test the running server: `scripts/run_server_with_token.sh` then hit health endpoint

### Key Dependencies to Watch
- `mcp` -- MCP SDK, check for protocol changes
- `fastapi` / `starlette` -- HTTP server framework
- `uvicorn` -- ASGI server
- `sqlalchemy` -- ORM, major version changes are breaking
- `gitpython` -- git operations for archive management
- `pydantic` -- data validation, v2 has breaking changes from v1

### Verification
- Server starts without errors
- Health check endpoint responds
- Agent registration works
- Message send/receive works
- JSONL archive writes correctly","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:19:45.282490046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:44:30.794894695Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-7z9","title":"Review server-side file reservation enforcement semantics","description":"send_message enforcement checks reservation patterns against mail archive paths (agents/*/inbox/outbox). Docs describe reservations as project file paths; review mismatch, decide intended enforcement surface, and update logic or docs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T04:12:09.220672324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:52:57.758727711Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:52:57.758685491Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-asnu","title":"Add _commit_direct EMFILE recovery path unit tests","description":"## Context
The _commit_direct function in storage.py has complex error handling for EMFILE (too many open files)
scenarios. During fresh-eyes review, a real fd leak bug was found and fixed (commit f2b03b9):
when EMFILE recovery creates a replacement Repo handle and the retry throws a non-OSError exception,
the replacement handle leaked because the finally block only closed the original repo.

Despite this critical path having a real bug that was caught in review, there are ZERO dedicated tests
for the EMFILE recovery logic. The only EMFILE-related tests are for the LRU repo cache eviction
(test_repo_cache.py), not for _commit_direct.

## Test cases to add (new file: tests/test_commit_emfile.py)

### 1. test_emfile_recovery_closes_original_repo
Mock Repo() to succeed, then _perform_commit to raise OSError(errno.EMFILE) on first call,
succeed on second. Verify:
- Original repo.close() called
- New repo used for second attempt
- Commit succeeds

### 2. test_emfile_recovery_non_oserror_retry_closes_replacement (THE BUG FIX TEST)
Mock _perform_commit to raise EMFILE on first call, then GitCommandError on second.
Verify:
- Original repo.close() called (by EMFILE recovery)
- Replacement repo.close() called (by finally block — this is the fix from f2b03b9)
- No fd leak

### 3. test_emfile_recovery_replacement_repo_constructor_fails
Mock Repo() to succeed first time, then raise InvalidGitRepositoryError on the EMFILE recovery
construction. Verify:
- Original repo.close() called (by EMFILE recovery before constructor)
- No attempt to close non-existent replacement
- Exception propagates cleanly

### 4. test_emfile_recovery_clears_cache_and_gc
Verify that during EMFILE recovery:
- clear_repo_cache() is called
- gc.collect() is called
- asyncio.sleep(0.05) is awaited
- Original repo is closed before new one is created

### 5. test_emfile_only_recovered_once
Verify EMFILE recovery only happens on attempt < 1 (first attempt). If EMFILE occurs on
attempt >= 1, it should be treated as fatal (re-raised via the "Other OSError" path).

### 6. test_commit_direct_finally_always_closes_repo
Verify that repo.close() is called in the finally block regardless of:
- AsyncFileLock raising
- Normal completion
- index.lock contention raising GitIndexLockError
- RuntimeError from loop exhaustion

## Implementation notes
- Use unittest.mock to mock Repo, _perform_commit, clear_repo_cache, gc.collect
- Use pytest-asyncio for async test functions
- Each test should create a real temporary git repo (tmpdir) for the Repo constructor
  OR mock Repo entirely
- Consider using MonkeyPatch to replace the _commit_direct internal Repo constructor

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/test_commit_emfile.py exists with all 6 test cases
- [ ] All 6 tests pass: pytest tests/test_commit_emfile.py -v
- [ ] test_emfile_recovery_non_oserror_retry_closes_replacement specifically exercises the f2b03b9 fix
- [ ] Tests mock at appropriate boundaries (Repo constructor, _perform_commit, clear_repo_cache)
- [ ] No tests require actual EMFILE conditions (all mocked)
- [ ] Existing 53+ tests still pass: pytest --tb=short
- [ ] Coverage of _commit_direct EMFILE paths improved (verify with pytest --cov if available)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:55.305268395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:05.040369618Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-bh8","title":"Investigate pathspec gitwildmatch deprecation warnings in tests","description":"Pytest emits DeprecationWarning from pathspec GitWildMatchPattern (gitwildmatch deprecated; use gitignore). Investigate dependency update or config to silence and ensure future compatibility.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:28:08.586160697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:12:50.963202911Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T00:12:50.963178685Z","close_reason":"Updated pathspec to gitignore","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-bh8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Replaced pathspec 'gitwildmatch' usage with 'gitignore' across app/cli/guard and benchmarks to remove deprecation warnings. Updated guard templates accordingly. Ran uvx ty check (pass) and pytest tests/test_pathspec_overlap.py (pass).","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:12:46Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-cc1n","title":"Add try/finally Repo handle tests for HTTP archive endpoints","description":"## Context
Three HTTP endpoints (archive_activity, archive_timeline, archive_network) were updated in
commit 81dd55f to use the defensive try/finally pattern for Repo handle cleanup:
  repo = None
  try:
      repo = GitRepo(str(repo_root))
      ...
  finally:
      if repo is not None:
          repo.close()

Two other endpoints (archive_commit, archive_guide) already had this pattern. But there are
no tests verifying that these endpoints actually close their Repo handles, even when exceptions
occur during rendering or data fetching.

## Test cases (add to tests/test_http_workers.py or new tests/test_http_archive.py)

### 1. test_archive_activity_closes_repo_on_success
Mock GitRepo and get_recent_commits. Call archive_activity endpoint.
Verify repo.close() called.

### 2. test_archive_activity_closes_repo_on_render_error
Mock GitRepo, then mock _render to raise. Verify repo.close() still called.

### 3. test_archive_timeline_closes_repo_on_success
Similar to above for timeline endpoint.

### 4. test_archive_network_closes_repo_on_success
Similar for network endpoint.

### 5. test_archive_commit_closes_repo_on_invalid_sha
Call archive_commit with invalid SHA. Verify repo.close() called despite ValueError.

### 6. test_archive_endpoints_no_repo_when_git_missing
When .git directory doesn't exist, endpoints should return error without creating Repo.

## Implementation notes
- Use httpx.AsyncClient with the FastAPI test client
- Mock git.Repo to track close() calls
- These tests verify the defensive pattern works, preventing fd leaks in the web viewer

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/test_http_archive.py exists with all 6 test cases
- [ ] All 6 tests pass: pytest tests/test_http_archive.py -v
- [ ] Tests verify repo.close() is called via mock assertion
- [ ] Tests verify repo.close() is called even when inner code raises
- [ ] Uses httpx.AsyncClient or equivalent test client
- [ ] Existing 53+ tests still pass: pytest --tb=short","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:30.933745870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:36.448108929Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cc1n","depends_on_id":"bd-asnu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:15.559842305Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-icd","title":"Add web UI routing support","description":"## Summary
Add web UI routing support to serve static files and handle SPA routing alongside MCP API endpoints.

## Context
- GitHub Issue #71 provides patch outline for web UI routing
- Need to serve static files from web/ directory
- Need /api/ prefix for MCP endpoints
- Need SPA fallback routing

## Requirements
1. Serve static files from web/ directory at root
2. Prefix all MCP tool endpoints with /api/
3. SPA fallback: non-API routes return index.html
4. CORS headers for local development

## Technical Approach
Using FastAPI/Starlette:
```python
app.mount("/api", mcp_router)
app.mount("/", StaticFiles(directory="web", html=True))

@app.exception_handler(404)
async def spa_fallback(request, exc):
    if not request.url.path.startswith("/api"):
        return FileResponse("web/index.html")
    raise exc
```

## Files to Modify  
- src/mcp_agent_mail/server.py - Add routing logic
- Create web/ directory structure

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Static files served at /
- [ ] MCP endpoints at /api/*
- [ ] SPA routes return index.html
- [ ] Development CORS working","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T05:03:13.490874378Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:45:57.592876552Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:45:57.592802813Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-p6n","title":"Integrate TOON into MCP Agent Mail (MCP server)","description":"## Goal
Add TOON (Token-Optimized Object Notation) output format to MCP Agent Mail tools/resources while keeping MCP JSON-RPC semantics intact.

## Current Implementation (2026-01-22)

### Output Envelope (when format=toon)
- Return a **compact envelope** instead of the original JSON payload:
```json
{
  "format": "toon",
  "data": "<toon string>",
  "meta": {
    "requested": "toon",
    "source": "param|default|implicit",
    "encoder": "tru",
    "toon_stats": {"json_tokens": 10, "toon_tokens": 5, "saved_tokens": 5, "saved_percent": 50.0}
  }
}
```
- The envelope is **JSON** (required by MCP), but the payload is **TOON** (token-optimized).
- When format is omitted and no defaults are set, outputs are unchanged (JSON as before).

### Format Resolution
Precedence: tool/resource `format` param > `MCP_AGENT_MAIL_OUTPUT_FORMAT` > `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT` > json.
Invalid values raise a ValueError (expected json|toon).

### Config / Env Vars
- `MCP_AGENT_MAIL_OUTPUT_FORMAT` - default format for tool outputs.
- `TOON_DEFAULT_FORMAT` - global fallback default for all tools/resources.
- `TOON_TRU_BIN` - override `tru` binary path (highest precedence).
- `TOON_BIN` - secondary override for `tru` path/command.
- `TOON_STATS` - enable `tru --stats` and parse stderr into `meta.toon_stats`.

## Implementation Summary (Done)
- **config**: Added defaults and env mapping in `src/mcp_agent_mail/config.py`.
- **format helpers**: `_normalize_output_format`, `_resolve_output_format` in `app.py`.
- **TOON encode**: `_run_toon_encode`, `_encode_payload_to_toon_sync` with graceful fallback.
- **tools**: Optional `format` param added to all MCP tools; wrapper applies TOON when requested.
- **resources**: `?format=toon` supported for resources; format param supported in resource handlers.
- **internal calls**: Macros and auto-handshake pass `format="json"` to avoid nested TOON.
- **docs**: README updated (Tools + Resources sections) and `resource://tooling` schemas include output format hints.

## Tests (Added)
- `tests/test_toon_formatting.py` (unit): tool & resource envelope + fallback.
- `tests/e2e/test_toon_format_e2e.py` (e2e): smoke coverage with detailed logging.

## Remaining Tasks
1) Run quality gates (requires explicit command approval):
   - `uvx ty check`
   - `uvx ruff check --fix --unsafe-fixes`
   - `pytest tests/test_toon_formatting.py tests/e2e/test_toon_format_e2e.py`
2) Ensure `tru` binary exists (build `toon_rust` or set `TOON_TRU_BIN` or `TOON_BIN`).
3) Manual verification:
   - `format=toon` returns envelope with TOON payload.
   - `format=json` returns unchanged shape.
   - TOON payload decodes to JSON-equivalent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- format=toon works for tools/resources without breaking JSON defaults.
- Envelope has minimal overhead and includes TOON payload + metadata.
- README/Tooling schemas document format usage.
- Unit tests + E2E pass with detailed logs.","notes":"Status update (2026-01-23):
- Resource URI templates now include {?format,...} so FastMCP accepts query params.
- Added test_resource_format_query_param_fastmcp.
- README now notes optional format query param for resources.
- Fixtures captured in /dp/toon_test_fixtures/real_world: mcp_agent_mail_health_check.{json,toon}, mcp_agent_mail_projects.{json,toon}.
- Quality gates: uvx ty check, uvx ruff check, pytest (5 tests) all pass.
- tru binary path resolved to /tmp/cargo-target/release/tru (CARGO_TARGET_DIR).
- NOTE: FastMCP does not accept resource://projects without a query param; use ?format=json or ?format=toon.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T21:49:12.340633763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:09:09.274146344Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:09:09.274126396Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-p6n","author":"QuietCanyon","text":"Updated description to tru naming (TOON_TRU_BIN/TOON_BIN, encoder=tru). Note: prior note about tr path is legacy; use tru path/env now.","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:40:01Z"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-p6n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Code review pass: fixed ty type errors in tests/test_concurrency_agents.py (require_dict_result helper + cast + boolean asserts). uvx ty check now passes; pytest tests/test_concurrency_agents.py passed (14 tests, pathspec deprecation warnings only). UBS python scan on tests/test_concurrency_agents.py: 0 warnings/critical. Ruff --fix --unsafe-fixes still pending (needs explicit command).","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:26:40Z"},{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-p6n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ran TOON tests:  passed (5 tests).  binary present at /home/ubuntu/.local/bin/tru. Remaining: ruff --fix --unsafe-fixes (needs explicit command) + manual TOON verification (format=toon/json parity).","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:30:39Z"},{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-p6n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Manual TOON verification (local server on :8770): resources/read for  returns JSON envelope with TOON payload;  returns normal JSON. Tool call  with  returns envelope in structuredContent + text. Note: resource payload still reports http.port=8765 from settings, even when server bound to 8770. Ruff unsafe fixes still pending.","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:07:04Z"},{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-p6n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Manual TOON verification (local server on :8770): resource://config/environment?format=toon returns JSON envelope with TOON payload; resource://config/environment?format=json returns normal JSON. Tool call health_check with format=toon returns envelope in structuredContent + text. Note: resource payload still reports http.port=8765 from settings even when server bound to 8770. Ruff unsafe fixes still pending.","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:07:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ycg","title":"Harden CLI ISO parsing for Z/offset timestamps","description":"CLI commands (e.g., file_reservations check) use datetime.fromisoformat without handling 'Z' suffix; add a helper to accept Z/offset and reuse for expiry comparisons and since filters.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T04:20:41.524010312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T21:53:09.444366371Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T21:53:09.444289776Z","close_reason":"Add ISO parser helper for Z/offset in CLI","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
